title: Add Server wizard hangs on vault + memory-rules install step
description: Adding a remote server via the Add Server wizard hangs during the step that installs the vault (clone) and memory/agent rules onto the new host. Never completes. Likely an SSH/git command waiting on input (host-key prompt, credential prompt, or missing deploy key) with no timeout. Needs: find the blocking command in ServerOnboarding.swift and make it non-interactive / time-bounded / surfaced.
notes: HANDOFF TO CODEX. Symptom: Add Server originally hung forever on step 5 'Clone the vault + memory rules' for mygatsa-production. Branch fix now covers both observed hang classes. Step 5: onboarding git uses GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0 + GIT_SSH_COMMAND with BatchMode/StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new/ConnectTimeout=15/ServerAliveInterval=10/CountMax=3, and runStep() watchdogs stop indefinite clone/verify hangs. Codex diagnostic 2026-07-08 from kepler against mygatsa-production (3.208.59.162): manual /tmp clone without keepalive completed exit=0 in 0s; clone with keepalive env completed exit=0 in 1s, so no box-side MSS/MTU or HTTPS fallback was implemented. Step 6 follow-up: Add Server then hung installing dev tools because toolStatus/installTools still used raw runShell without watchdogs, and apt-get update on mygatsa-production fails non-interactively on the Ondrej PHP PPA release-info label change unless --allow-releaseinfo-change is passed. Fix: tool checks, apt package installs, and CLI installer calls now use runStep timeouts; apt uses DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive, Dpkg::Use-Pty=0, and update --allow-releaseinfo-change before install -y. Verification: xcodegen generate succeeded; DEVELOPER_DIR=/Applications/Xcode-beta.app/Contents/Developer xcodebuild -project AronnaxIDE.xcodeproj -scheme AronnaxIDE -destination 'platform=macOS' -derivedDataPath /private/tmp/AronnaxIDE-DerivedData build succeeded on kepler. Do NOT close until Kam confirms Add Server works on-device.
close_reason: Confirmed by Kameron after shipping d477c8a: Add Server flow now completes and installed app is updated. Follow-up UI polish tracked separately.

title: A server is ONE project at a path, not a folder to scan
description: Treating a server like the hub made the sidebar list the project's subfolders and run Coding from the wrong one, and required manual post-wizard vault/project paths. Now: per-server project directory; wizard captures it + sets vault+project paths on finish; sidebar shows the one project dir (titled 'Project'); Coding/Vault/Beads/Git target it.
close_reason: Reworked server-project model across AppSettings/wizard/sidebar/settings.

title: Coding tab: launch Claude + Codex in auto mode (bypass permission prompts)
description: Launch both agents in the Coding pane with their auto/bypass flags so the build/review pipeline runs hands-off on kepler. Claude: bypass-permissions flag; Codex: full-auto/bypass-approvals flag. Edit the Coding pane agent launch commands.
close_reason: Per-agent permission-mode dropdowns shipped (commit 2a22052)

title: Coding Both-view: Codex tab scroll-back locks + paste lands in Claude tab
description: In the Both view, when the Codex tab is active you cannot scroll back up in Codex (you can in Claude), and pasting into Codex appears in the Claude tab. Likely shared terminal/scroll state or paste/focus routing between the two agent panes.
notes: ROOT CAUSE: Codex doesn't grab the mouse and runs on the normal screen (tmux mouse_any=0, alt_screen=0), so its history is tmux scrollback; with tmux mouse off the wheel never reached it. Claude grabs mouse + alt-screen so it scrolls itself. FIX: tmux set -g mouse on (+ persisted in ~/.tmux.conf). App's drag-select is unaffected (allowMouseReporting=false keeps drags local).
close_reason: Paste routing + Codex scroll both fixed (commits 2a22052, f58913a); user confirmed scroll works.

title: Coding pane runs agents on the project's host (servers, not just the mini)
description:
Today CodingPane hardcodes host: settings.hub, so the pretty agent chat only runs on kepler. Make it run claude/codex on whatever host the selected project lives on (gatsa-staging, pbisario, etc.).

Project struct (Fleet.swift) already has hostID + path, so the model supports it. Gaps:
1. Selection must carry the project's host, not just a path (AppSettings.selectedProjectPath is a bare String; panes use settings.hub). Add selected-project host resolution.
2. Server projects need to exist in the sidebar: either register them (host + path + name, persisted) or discover per-host. Hub still auto-discovers projectsRoot.
3. Route CodingPane.start/sync to the selected project's host + path instead of settings.hub.
4. Decide scope: Coding pane first; Terminal already has a host switcher; Vault/Beads/Git stay hub-pinned (memory + tasks live on the mini per the unified-memory design).

Verify: select a server project, Coding pane attaches claude on that server in the project dir.
close_reason: Active-host selector built: project panes (Coding/Vault/Beads/Git) follow the selected host; agents run on the server, kepler is the fallback.

title: Restructure vault: AI_OS root + AI_OS/Projects for code
description: Moved the vault to ~/Documents/AI_OS (memory at root) with project folders under ~/Documents/AI_OS/Projects. IDE: workdir default updated + migration; projects = immediate subfolders of <vault>/Projects (repo or not); vault note scan excludes Projects/. Updated CTX-project-index paths on the mini.
close_reason: Vault restructured + IDE wired to new layout; verified discovery + vault exclusion.

title: SSH write guardrails (protected / read-only / confirm)
description: Per-host Protected (Terminal confirm + red banner) and Read-only (block app writes) flags, plus a global Confirm-before-every-write toggle. Wired into Terminal (protected) and Vault/Git/Beads (read-only disables write controls; confirm-writes prompts before save/commit/push/checkout/beads mutations).
close_reason: All 3 guardrails wired: protected terminal, read-only blocks, confirm-writes.

title: Browser auto-preview: agents push a URL/port into the Browser pane
description: Core intent of the Browser pane: when Claude/Codex build something (a dev server / HTML), it should auto-appear in the Browser, like Claude desktop. Mechanism: the agent writes a URL or port to a per-project signal file (.miniide-preview in the project dir on the hub); the app watches that file for the selected project and, when it changes, forwards the port (if localhost) and loads it. Add an Auto toggle.
close_reason: App side built: PreviewWatcher + auto-load. Agent convention: write URL/port to .miniide-preview.

title: Terminal auto-reconnects on connection drop
description: The terminal ran ssh once and, on failure (mini asleep / network blip / outage), printed an error and sat dead until a manual Reconnect. Now it auto-reconnects with exponential backoff (2,4,8,16s, capped, max 6 tries), forcing a fresh ControlMaster each retry. Clean exits (user 'exit' / tmux detach, exit 0) are left alone. Verified against SwiftTerm: terminate() is synchronous and callback-less (cancels the process monitor), so only genuine drops reach processTerminated.
close_reason: Implemented + verified (xcodebuild + codex clean).

title: Split view freezes the app
description: Opening a split froze the app. Split code (ContentView) has no loop/blocking call, so likely a runtime combo (two heavy panes / two SwiftTerm clients on the same tmux session causing a resize war, or duplicate WKWebViews). Need: which tab the user split from. Investigate.
notes: Replaced HSplitView with pure-SwiftUI SplitContainer (no NSSplitView). Also adds vertical/bottom split + AnyLayout identity preservation + draggable divider + pane border. Codex clean, build green. Awaiting GUI confirm that unsplit no longer freezes.
close_reason: Confirmed fixed; re-close on main after merge (Dolt revert)

title: TerminalPane auto-reconnect on wake
description: On reconnect signal: terminate stale ssh, closeMaster(host), restart ssh -> tmux new-session -A re-attaches the same session. Keepalives (ServerAlive) already in SSHManager.
notes: Codex review (3 passes): (1) closeMaster ran on first attach -> gated to reconnects; (2) ContentView preview missing WakeObserver env object -> added; (3) multi-pane master reset race -> SSHManager.resetMasterOnce(host,generation) closes master once per wake signal. Pass 3 clean.

title: WakeObserver: NSWorkspace wake + network path
description: Observe NSWorkspace.didWakeNotification and NWPathMonitor (unsatisfied->satisfied). Publish a reconnect signal on the main actor; injected via environment.
notes: Codex review (3 passes): (1) gitignore build-*/ DerivedData dirs; (2) satisfied->satisfied route changes (VPN/roam/handoff) were suppressed -> switched to NWPath signature (interfaces+gateways) comparison; (3) dead-code until wired -> handled by 6mt.2. WakeObserver = NSWorkspace.didWake + NWPathMonitor, @MainActor, edge-triggered.

title: TerminalPane: SwiftTerm NSViewRepresentable
description: Embed SwiftTerm LocalProcessTerminalView; run ssh -t <mini> -- tmux new-session -A -s main.
notes: Codex review (4 passes): caught and fixed 3x[P2] — (1) ssh process leak on view dismantle: added dismantleNSView; (2) tmux session name interpolated unquoted: exposed SSHManager.shellEscaped, quote the name; (3) stale-pid race: terminate() only if process.running. Also added SSHManager.loginShellArguments (login shell so kepler PATH has tmux/claude/codex). Pass 4 clean. Build green. Live tmux attach verified against kepler from CLI.

title: SSHManager: ssh Process + ControlMaster
description: Spawn system ssh with ControlMaster=auto/ControlPersist; per-host shared connection; helper to run remote commands.
notes: Codex review (3 passes, codex review --uncommitted): caught and fixed 3x[P2] — (1) remote argv boundaries: shell-quote each token in run(), runShell() is the explicit raw-shell path; (2) ControlPath could exceed macOS ~104B socket limit for long host ids: FNV-1a hashed socket name; (3) cancelled task left ssh running: withTaskCancellationHandler terminates the process. Pass 3 clean. Build green.

title: Create private GitHub repo + push
description: gh repo create mini-ide (private, kameronpence); push scaffold + .beads.

title: Xcode project scaffold + SwiftTerm
description: xcodegen project.yml; SwiftUI app shell (sidebar + tabbed workspace); SwiftTerm SPM dep; entitlements; build green.

title: codex login (ChatGPT kpence73)
description: INTERACTIVE: user runs 'codex login' against ChatGPT plan. Needed for two-agent workflow.
notes: codex already logged in via ChatGPT (codex login status confirms). Verified IDE-48l.4.

title: Initialize beads + seed backlog
description: bd init; create M0-M12 milestone epics with blocks deps + near-term tasks.

title: Install bd + codex via curl
description: beads via official curl script; codex release binary via curl to ~/.local/bin.

title: Local git repo init
description: git init in project dir; user.email kameronpence@gmail.com (personal GitHub).

title: M1 — SSHManager + Terminal pane
description: Process-based ssh with ControlMaster multiplexing; SwiftTerm terminal attaching to tmux 'main' on the mini.
close_reason: 3/3 children complete — M1 done (SSHManager, TerminalPane, host config)

title: M0 — Project bootstrap & scaffold
description: Local git repo, install bd+codex via curl, Xcode project scaffold with SwiftTerm, beads tracker seeded, private GitHub repo created and pushed.

title: Gate interactive/stream/port-forward spawns on async Tailscale resolution
description: Follow-up to the Tailscale connection fix. run/runShell/isReachable now await SSHManager.ensureTailscaleResolved so command paths reach kepler over Tailscale on first attempt. The interactive terminal (TerminalPane.launch), coding pane (CodingPane.launch), log stream (LogStreamController.startRaw), and port forward (PortForwardManager.open) build ssh args SYNCHRONOUSLY and rely on the launch-time prewarm being warm — a cold-launch-straight-into-terminal race (at work) could still hit the LAN IP on the very first attempt before falling back on reconnect. Fix: make those spawn paths await ensureTailscaleResolved(for:) before building args (convert the sync launch funcs to async + update callers). Needs on-device testing since it touches the core terminal connect path.

title: Harden kepler connection: prefer Tailscale address over LAN IP
description: AronnaxIDE on the MacBook connects to kepler via the 'kepler' ssh alias, whose HostName is a LAN IP (10.63.1.233, now drifted to 10.63.1.36). Works at home, fails everywhere else even with Tailscale on, because ssh dials the LAN address. The iOS app works anywhere because it uses kepler's Tailscale IP (100.106.82.21). Harden: the app should reach kepler via its Tailscale address by default (or try Tailscale then fall back to LAN) so it connects from any network without hand-editing ~/.ssh/config, and not break when the LAN IP changes. Immediate workaround given to Kam: point the Mac's 'kepler' ssh alias HostName at 100.106.82.21.

title: Replace sidebar reorder chevrons with drag-and-drop
description: The temporary up/down chevrons in the Working on and Projects sidebar lists work, but the UI looks bad. Replace them with native drag-and-drop reorder behavior for both lists. Preserve persisted ordering for hosts and projects, support keyboard/accessible fallback if appropriate, and keep row selection behavior clean.
acceptance_criteria: Working on hosts can be reordered by drag-and-drop; hub Projects can be reordered by drag-and-drop; order persists across relaunch; no visible up/down chevron clutter remains; selecting rows still works without accidental reordering.

title: AronnaxIDE: per-project GitHub account selection (personal vs GATSA org)
description: Need a way to tell AronnaxIDE which GitHub account/remote a project uses: Kam's personal GitHub for personal projects vs the GATSA org for GATSA projects. Was believed to be automatic but it is not — git push/PR operations need to target the right account/remote per project. Add explicit per-project selection (and/or reliable auto-detection from the project's git remote/org).
notes: COMPLETE & merged to main (PRs #7 feat, #8 fix, #9 fix). Per-project GitHub account picker in Git/Deploy pane: enumerates accounts from ~/.ssh/config github aliases (incl Include files), resolves real account names via 'ssh -T git@<alias>' (github.com→kameronpence, github-gatsa→GATSA), switches by rewriting origin to git@<alias>:owner/repo (converts HTTPS→SSH), hides deploy-key aliases (except the repo's own). Alias-safe parseRemote fixed owner capsule + gh Actions for github-gatsa repos. Codex-reviewed across all 3 PRs (13+ findings resolved). AWAITING Kam final on-device confirm before close (per dont-close-until-confirmed rule).

title: iOS: cannot scroll claude/codex terminal to see scrollback above the top
description: In the AronnaxIOS app, the claude/codex terminal window can't be scrolled up to view output that has already scrolled off the top of the screen. Two-finger scroll emits wheel events to the remote (SwiftTerm allowMouseReporting=false, plain shell scrollback owned by the scroll view). Need to make the scrollback actually reachable on the phone. Related to TerminalSurface.swift / AronnaxTerminalView.swift.
notes: VERIFIED FIX (sim, 2026-07-06): root cause was the gesture never firing (panGestureRecognizer.addTarget starved -> p=0 on device) AND wheel sent as hardcoded SGR bytes. Fix mirrors macOS ClipboardTerminalView: own UIPanGestureRecognizer + UIGestureRecognizerDelegate(simultaneous) + terminal.sendEvent(buttonFlags:x:y:). Automated XCUITest drag in the Simulator: pan 0->162, wheel 0->78, tmux scrolled back through Claude history (screenshot confirmed). Diagnostic + UITest scaffolding removed; clean build renders + succeeds. Committed locally on branch fix/ios-scroll-sendevent (NOT pushed). NEXT: Kam deploys to device from kepler (phone plugged in) and confirms one-finger drag scrolls; then push+merge and close.
close_reason: One-finger drag scroll verified working on device by Kam (2026-07-06). Fix: own delegated UIPanGestureRecognizer + terminal.sendEvent wheel encoding.

title: iOS app: phone→kepler SSH times out over cellular (Tailscale data path)
description: AronnaxIOS now builds/signs/installs/renders and attempts to connect, but SSH to kepler (100.106.82.21:22) times out over T-Mobile cellular. PROVEN: kepler firewall off, sshd listening, Tailscale ACL allows the phone on all ports (netmap packet filter: 100.64.0.0/11 -> 0.0.0.0/0 all ports), kepler->phone tailscale ping works (direct 85ms and via DERP). The block is DIRECTIONAL: phone->kepler:22 TCP never completes (Citadel connectTimeout 30s fires) while kepler->phone works. Likely a Tailscale data-path issue over cellular CGNAT (disco/ping works, WireGuard TCP data doesn't), phone-side. NEXT: (1) on the phone, use Tailscale app diagnostics to test reaching kepler; check if Tailscale drops/backgrounds on cellular. (2) App robustness: add auto-retry + shorter connectTimeout to SSHTerminalSession.connect() so it recovers when the path is briefly up (Citadel SSHClientSettings.connectTimeout exists). (3) Consider whether the old 'works on WiFi' build used a LAN IP (bypassing Tailscale). Build pipeline on kepler now works: xcodegen scheme gen + team 46W4LYLT6R + SWIFT_ENABLE_EXPLICIT_MODULES=NO; build via -scheme AronnaxIOS, sign via Xcode GUI (codesign errSecInternalComponent over SSH).
notes: UPDATE (end of session): Network.framework swap IMPLEMENTED — vendored Citadel to ThirdParty/Citadel, added swift-nio-transport-services, patched ClientSession.connect() to use NIOTSConnectionBootstrap+NIOTSEventLoopGroup under #if canImport(Network). Compiles clean (generic iOS, CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO). project.yml points Citadel at the local path. Xcode needed quit+reopen to load the local package (was 'Missing package product Citadel'; cleared stale Package.resolved). Installed to phone via Xcode Run. BUT: still not connecting in testing — app cycled 'Connecting (3..39)' then blank screen; kepler-side watch saw ZERO incoming SSH in 90s. Unclear if tested on cellular vs wifi (user switching networks, exhausted). Blank screen = the debugger-launch artifact (must launch from home screen, not Xcode Run). NEXT SESSION: (1) install via Xcode Run, STOP debugger, launch from home screen (avoid blank), (2) verify NIOTS is actually the runtime path (add a log/status), (3) test WiFi first to confirm the patched build connects at all (rule out patch regression), then cellular, (4) if NIOTS build fails on wifi too, the patch has a runtime bug — check NIOTSConnectionBootstrap channelInitializer + connect. All changes uncommitted in working tree (retry in SSHTerminalSession.swift, project.yml build fixes, ThirdParty/Citadel). Not pushed.

title: Move standalone MySQL database to the new native MySQL 9 server
description: Kam wants a project's database (on a standalone MySQL) migrated to the newly-running native MySQL 9 on kepler (127.0.0.1:3306). Which standalone MySQL and which database were not pinned down this session. NEXT: get from Kam — the source (container name / port / host of the standalone MySQL) and the database name — then plan the dump + import. Do NOT act until confirmed (destructive DB op).

title: New Project wizard with MySQL provisioning
description: AronnaxIDE has no in-app way to create a project — folders are only discovered. Add a New Project wizard (modeled on AddServerWizard/ServerOnboarding): name input, create dir under projectsRoot on the hub, git init, provision a MySQL db + scoped user on kepler's native MySQL 9 (root via ~/.my.cnf, app never holds root pw), write a gitignored .env with the scoped-user creds (Laravel-style DB_ keys), then rescan. Standard: no Docker/SQLite; every project uses kepler MySQL with its own db+user.
notes: Built + compiles + CodeRabbit-reviewed (concealed-clipboard fix applied), committed locally 52bf276 (unpushed). PENDING: (1) Kam creates mode-600 ~/.my.cnf on kepler with root creds so provisioning can auth as root; (2) end-to-end verify on the Mac after aronnax-ship.
close_reason: Reversed — the wizard is for remote servers, not local kepler projects. Feature branch deleted (never pushed).

title: Add 'kepler root' row to deselect project (panes follow /Users/kepler)
description: Currently selecting a project locks all panes to that project dir, and the only way to run claude/codex at the kepler home root is a host-toggle hack. Add a selectable 'kepler root' row at the top of the sidebar project list that sets selectedProjectPath to /Users/kepler, so Terminal/Coding/Browser/Beads/Git all operate at host home. Vault stays on the AI_OS Obsidian vault. Guard the auto-selector so root selection sticks.
close_reason: Implemented 'kepler root' sidebar row; deselects project so all panes follow /Users/kepler. Built + CodeRabbit-reviewed, committed locally (unpushed).

title: M0: iOS Citadel->SwiftTerm->kepler terminal spike (code compiles; verify live)
description: Part of IDE-g42. iOS target + Citadel SSH + SwiftTerm bridge compiles. Remaining: add the app pubkey to kepler authorized_keys, then run (simulator or device) to confirm a live kepler shell.
close_reason: Verified live in iOS Simulator: connected to kepler over Tailscale via Citadel, interactive PTY shell rendered in SwiftTerm (screenshot shows kepler@Keplers-Mac-mini prompt).

title: iOS AronnaxIDE — Terminal + Coding (mobile companion)
description: A native iOS app version of AronnaxIDE with only the Terminal and Coding panes, to monitor/steer agents on kepler from the phone over Tailscale. Same repo, new iOS target, shared logic (AgentController command strings, mode enums, host model). Transport is Citadel (pure-Swift SSH) instead of system ssh, since iOS can't spawn subprocesses. UI is iOS-specific (segmented Terminal/Coding, key-accessory bar). Reaches kepler at tailscale 100.106.82.21; AWS servers via ProxyJump-through-kepler in v1.1.

title: Add-Server wizard: auto-install dev tools + clearer key/GitHub steps
description: Wizard tools step dead-ended on a manual 'install zsh then retry'. Now it auto-installs missing tools (zsh/tmux via the box package manager; claude/codex/bd/cr via official installers), re-checks, and flags any that still need sign-in. Foothold step got real numbered instructions; GitHub step shows the key fingerprint + handles 'key already in use', and reminds it's personal GitHub not GATSA.
close_reason: Built into ServerOnboarding + AddServerWizard.

title: Vault tab: show each project's docs on kepler (was pruning all of Projects/)
description: Vault pane pruned the entire Projects/ subtree, so project docs (CLAUDE.md, DECISIONS.md, ROADMAP.md, logs/) were invisible on the hub. Now prune only code/build dirs so docs show without the dependency-README flood.
close_reason: RemoteFS.listMarkdown now prunes code-noise dirs instead of all of Projects/.

title: Search in Vault, Beads, Git (Logs already filters)
description: Vault: filter the file list by note name. Beads: filter issues by id/title/type. Git: search commit messages across history (git log --grep). Logs already has a substring filter.
close_reason: Search added to Vault/Beads/Git; Logs already filtered.

title: Git/Deploy branch switcher (+ optional browser preview link)
description: Git panel shows only the current branch read-only. Add a branch picker (checkout on the hub) and optionally tie a selected branch to a browser preview.
close_reason: Branch picker + checkout on the hub; dirty-tree safe.

title: Host configurability in Settings (make it distributable)
description: Host is semi-hardcoded to kepler. For GitHub distribution: editable hosts in Settings (add/remove, hostname/user/reach, pick the hub), drop the hardcoded default.
close_reason: Editable + persisted hosts in Settings; merges with ssh-config discovery.

title: M11 — Host health panel
description: At-a-glance fleet health: each host's reachability (ssh probe, bounded) and, for the hub, which tmux sessions are alive (main + the per-project agent sessions). Auto-refresh + manual. New Health tab.
close_reason: Implemented + verified (xcodebuild + codex clean).

title: M9 — Remote log viewer (stream + filter)
description: Logs tab is a placeholder. Build a live remote log viewer: pick a source (tail a file path, or run a streaming command like pm2 logs / docker logs -f / log stream) on the hub, stream it over ssh into a scrollable monospaced view with live substring filtering, pause/resume, clear, and auto-scroll. Cap buffered lines.
close_reason: Implemented + verified (xcodebuild + codex).

title: Global project selection drives all panes + app icon
description: Click a project in the left sidebar to set a single global selection (AppSettings.selectedProjectPath); Coding, Vault, Beads, and Git all follow it. Removed per-panel project dropdowns. Agents run per-project (session keyed by workdir). Vault saves unsaved edits before switching, locks the editor, reverts the sidebar on save failure, and invalidates stale loads. Wired CFBundleIconName so the AronnaxIDE app icon shows.
close_reason: Implemented and verified (xcodebuild + codex review clean).

title: Sidebar usage panel: Claude /usage + Codex /status via tmux scrape
description: Poll agent usage by driving a throwaway tmux session on the hub: launch claude/codex, clear startup prompts, send /usage (Claude) or /status (Codex, read-only — NOT /usage which consumes a reset), capture-pane, parse %/reset (5h + weekly). Claude reports % used, Codex % left — normalize. Show two mini gauges at the bottom of the sidebar + refresh button + last-updated. Both probes are free (no token cost). ~15s/refresh so run in background.
close_reason: Completed + verified; re-close on main after merge

title: BeadsController + panel: list/ready/status, project picker, refresh
description: Run bd --json over SSH against per-project .beads dbs on the hub. Discover project dirs under agentWorkdir; project picker; list issues with status/priority/type; filter (all/ready/open/active/blocked/closed); refresh; loading+error states. bd lives in ~/.local/bin (off non-login PATH) so prepend PATH and cd into the project dir.
close_reason: Completed + verified; re-close on main after merge

title: Terminal scroll + Cmd-C copy broken by tmux mouse layer
description: In Coding terminals: scroll doesn't work (agent mouse-mode isn't propagated through tmux to SwiftTerm, so the IDE-d56 event-monitor fix never triggers) and Cmd-C doesn't copy a drag-selection. Both stem from the tmux mouse layer. Proposed fix: tmux 'set -g mouse on' + 'set -g set-clipboard on' (+ allow-passthrough) so tmux handles scroll AND drag-select copies to the system clipboard via OSC52 — verify SwiftTerm honors OSC52 clipboard writes. Supersedes IDE-d56's approach.
notes: Fix: allowMouseReporting=false → drag = local SwiftTerm selection → Cmd-C copies to system clipboard for both agents; scroll preserved (wheel forwarded via terminal.sendEvent). Codex clean, build green. Awaiting user GUI confirmation.
close_reason: Confirmed fixed; re-close on main after merge (Dolt revert)

title: RemoteFS: list/read/atomic-write vault files over ssh
description: File access to the vault on a host via the shared ssh master: list *.md (find), read (cat), atomic write (cat to temp via stdin, then mv). Needs SSHManager stdin support for writes.
close_reason: RemoteFS list/read/atomic-write + SSHManager stdin; codex clean; build succeeded

title: VaultPane: file tree + markdown editor (load/save via RemoteFS) + preview
description: Sidebar/list of vault markdown files; selecting one loads it (RemoteFS.read) into a SwiftUI markdown editor with edit + live preview; save writes back (RemoteFS.write). Replaces the Vault placeholder.
close_reason: Vault list/editor/preview + save; robust concurrency; codex clean; build succeeded

title: Can't scroll up in Coding (Claude/Codex) terminals — enable tmux mouse mode
description: Agent terminals run in tmux's alternate screen, which has no terminal scrollback, so SwiftTerm scroll does nothing. Fix: enable tmux 'mouse on' so trackpad scroll routes into tmux/the agent. kepler ~/.tmux.conf change (not app code).
close_reason: Scroll-wheel forwarded to alt-screen agents via event monitor; codex clean; build succeeded

title: Chat (web) pane: claude.ai + ChatGPT sub-tabs; rename agent pane to 'Coding'
description: New 'Chat' workspace tab hosting two WebChatViews (claude.ai, chatgpt.com) with a sub-tab switcher. Rename the existing CLI-agent tab/pane from 'Chat' to 'Coding' (WorkspaceTab + label). Wire the new Chat surface into ContentView/WorkspaceSurface.
close_reason: M5 complete + merged to main (re-close after branch-checkout reverted Dolt)

title: WebChatView: persistent-login WKWebView for a fixed chat site
description: WKWebView pinned to one URL (claude.ai or chatgpt.com) using a persistent WKWebsiteDataStore so the subscription login survives relaunches. Minimal chrome: back + reload (no URL bar). Reusable for both sites.
close_reason: M5 complete + merged to main (re-close after branch-checkout reverted Dolt)

title: Codex chat: input box not clearing/submitting on Enter in agent terminal
description: In the Chat pane's Codex terminal, typing + Enter leaves the text in the composer and further typing appends to it (input box not cleared/submitted). Claude's chat reportedly works in the same SwiftTerm->ssh->tmux stack, so likely Codex-TUI-specific redraw/key handling under tmux. Investigate TERM / tmux passthrough / Enter encoding.
close_reason: Not a MiniIDE/terminal bug — Codex's slash-command menu retains unrecognized input (e.g. /useage matches no command, so Enter is a no-op and text stays). Claude has no slash menu so it sends normally. Clear with Ctrl+U. Confirmed Codex-specific behavior.

title: PortForwardManager: ssh -L tunnels to mini localhost; open in browser
description: Manage ssh -N -L localPort:localhost:remotePort <hub> tunnels (open/close/track, reuse ControlMaster). Browser pane gets a control to forward a mini localhost port and load http://localhost:<localPort>.
close_reason: M4 complete + merged to main (re-close after branch-checkout reverted Dolt state)

title: BrowserPane: WKWebView + URL/navigation bar
description: WKWebView wrapped for SwiftUI with a nav bar: back/forward/reload, editable URL field (Enter to load), URL normalization (add http:// if missing). BrowserModel (ObservableObject + WKNavigationDelegate) tracks canGoBack/Forward and current URL. Replaces the Browser placeholder.
close_reason: M4 complete + merged to main (re-close after branch-checkout reverted Dolt state)

title: Chat pane: Claude + Codex side by side (Both mode)
description: ChatPane switcher becomes Claude / Codex / Both. 'Both' shows the two agent terminals in an HSplitView (each labeled), both attached to their own tmux session simultaneously.
close_reason: Claude/Codex/Both switcher; Both = HSplitView of both agent terminals; codex clean; build succeeded

title: Split workspace — two panes side by side
description: Workspace is single-tab (one pane at a time). Add a split: left pane (top tab bar) + optional right pane in an HSplitView, each choosing its own surface; a split toggle opens/closes the right pane, which has its own surface picker + close button. Enables Terminal+Chat, Chat+Browser, etc.
close_reason: Split workspace via HSplitView; stable left pane; codex clean; build succeeded

title: Live connection status + Reconnect button in status bar
description: The bottom status bar hardcodes 'Disconnected' (gray). Make it reflect real hub reachability: ConnectionMonitor probes ssh true on a timer; status bar shows green/Connected, red/Disconnected, yellow/Connecting; add a Reconnect button that drives WakeObserver's reconnect signal (panes re-attach). Add ConnectTimeout so a down hub flips to Disconnected promptly instead of hanging.
close_reason: Live status + Reconnect; codex clean after 3 passes; build succeeded

title: Remove redundant chat input bar — type directly in the agent terminal
description: The ChatPane's bottom 'Message <agent>' TextField + Send button (which injected text via tmux send-keys) duplicates the live SwiftTerm terminal, where the user already types into the agent's real prompt. Remove the input bar and the now-unused AgentController.sendKeys; keep the agent switcher + terminal.
close_reason: Input bar + sendKeys removed; codex clean; build succeeded

title: Cmd-C/V/A clipboard shortcuts in terminal panes
description: SwiftUI's default Edit menu doesn't reliably route Cmd-C/Cmd-V/Cmd-A into the embedded SwiftTerm views. Add a LocalProcessTerminalView subclass that overrides performKeyEquivalent: Cmd-C copies the selection (gated on selectionActive), Cmd-V pastes, Cmd-A selects all; Ctrl-C/V stay control codes. Use it in TerminalPane and the Chat agent terminal.
close_reason: ClipboardTerminalView subclass; codex clean (incl. caps-lock fix); build succeeded

title: ChatPane: terminal + chat input + agent switcher; wire into ContentView
description: SwiftUI pane: SwiftTerm view attached to the active agent window; chat-style TextField that sends via AgentController.sendKeys; segmented Claude/Codex switcher that re-attaches and retargets input. Replaces the Chat placeholder in ContentView.
close_reason: AgentController + ChatPane; codex review clean; build succeeded

title: AgentController: claude/codex tmux windows + send-keys input
description: Manage two tmux windows on the hub: agent-claude (runs 'claude') and agent-codex (runs 'codex'), launched in the AI_OS vault. Expose ensure-window, attach command, and sendKeys(text) via tmux send-keys to the active agent. Builds on SSHManager.
close_reason: AgentController + ChatPane; codex review clean; build succeeded

title: Connection/host config plumbing
description: Mini host alias/user/identity in AppSettings; read ~/.ssh/config; wire into SSHManager.
notes: WIP — NOT verified-complete. SSHConfigParser + AppSettings wiring done (uncommitted). Codex review: pass1 caught [P2] single-alias-only + [P2] hub user regress (fixed); pass2 caught [P2] inline-comment not stripped (fixed); pass3 verification HIT CODEX USAGE LIMIT (resets 10:01pm). Re-run codex review --uncommitted after reset; if clean, commit + close.
close_reason: SSHConfigParser + AppSettings wiring; codex clean; build succeeded

title: M2 — Keepalives + reconnect-on-wake
description: ServerAliveInterval keepalives; WakeObserver restarts ssh session + port-forwards on NSWorkspace.didWake.

title: Allow designating a custom host as the hub
description: Codex review flagged that addHost always sets isHub=false and settings.hub resolves to the hardcoded kepler alias, so the vault/beads/git/agent panes can't be pointed at a different hub. This is a design boundary today (the app assumes kepler is the hub with AI_OS/bd/agents set up), so swapping hubs needs: a hub toggle in the add-host form, single-hub enforcement (clear the discovered hub's isHub), and confirming the new hub actually has the vault/bd/agents. Deferred from the m8-projects branch review.
close_reason: Not needed — single-hub setup by design (only kepler; no second mini to swap to).

title: Health panel polish: readable names, tunnels, per-project git
description: Health shows agent-claude-<hash> (unreadable). Map hashes to project names; show active port-forwards/tunnels and per-project git dirty/ahead-behind.
close_reason: Readable session names (hash→project) + active tunnels section.

title: Persist workspace split layout across launches
description: M12 polish leftover: persist the WorkspaceModel pane tree (splits + each pane's tab) and restore it on launch, so the user's multi-pane layout survives a relaunch. Deferred from M12 — needs Codex review when building.
close_reason: Layout persisted via Codable PaneTree in UserDefaults.
